instructions.instruction: Put the flour, salt, MSG and yeast into a bowl and whisk briefly to combine.
Add the water and mix. The mixture will be quite wet, somewhere between a batter and a dough. Ensure fully mixed, cover and leave for 1-2 hours.

Coat your hands and work surface with olive oil and pour out the mixture. Draw it from the edges to the centre, turn 90 degrees and repeat. The dough will be very sticky at this stage. Repeat this fold twice more at hourly intervals (3 folds total).

Oil two roasting tins and separate batter into equal 900g lumps, placing one onto each tin. Fold again—it should be less sticky now and starting to take shape. Cover and leave overnight in a cool place to rise.
Next morning, fold a final time and then push the dough down and out on the tin using splayed fingers or a pizza roller. Leave to proof for another hour.
Top each focaccia with flaky salt and your choice of toppings: chilli flakes, rosemary sprigs coated in oil, or whatever you like.
Heat your oven as hot as it will go and add both trays. Once the bread is in, turn the oven down to 200°C (fan).
After 10 minutes, turn the trays and bake for another 15 minutes until golden brown. Internal temperature should read over 90°C.
Pour a couple of generous glugs of best quality olive oil over each bread while still hot. Remove to wire racks to cool for about an hour.
